front: Work: the protagonist of this novella is a member of Gang/Squad 104, a group of zeks in a Siberian labor camp
back: One Day in the Life of Ivan Denisovich (Solzhenitsyn)
work: One Day in the Life of Ivan Denisovich

front: Work: Alyosha the Baptist maintains his faith despite harsh conditions in this novella; he is the protagonist's roommate

front: Work: this nonfiction work about the Soviet gulag system is based on the testimony of 227 survivors
Work: this work is dedicated 'to all those who did not live to tell it'
Work: an epigraph by poet Wilhelm Muller notes 'How heavy are the stones themselves' in this nonfiction work
Work: a chapter called 'The White Kitten' tells of Georgi Tenno, who escapes but is recaptured after refusing to kill villagers
Work: in this work, the author compares Shakespeare's worst villains (Macbeth, Iago) to 'little lambs' because they had no ideology
Work: Oleg Kostoglotov has romances with nurse Zoya and doctor Vera Gangart in a hospital in Kazakhstan
Work: on the day he leaves the hospital, the protagonist visits a zoo and reads about the 'senseless cruelty' of an evil man who blinded a monkey
Work: teenager Dyomka has his leg amputated; this novel ends with the protagonist writing letters to Dyomka, Zoya, and Vera
Work: set in a sharashka, practitioners of 'phonoscopy' compare spectrographs leading to the arrest of diplomat Innokenty Volodin
Work: Gleb Nerzhin befriends Communist Lev Rubin, while mathematician Sologdin betrays his integrity for freedom
Author: this author waited four years to receive his Nobel Prize; Sweden refused to let him lecture at its Moscow embassy
Author: this author gave the 1978 Harvard commencement address on 'A World Split Apart,' discussing the West's decline and loss of civil courage
Author: this author's Nobel lecture quotes 'ONE WORD OF TRUTH SHALL OUTWEIGH THE WHOLE WORLD'
Author: August 1914 is the first 'knot' of this author's cycle The Red Wheel; it follows Colonel Vorotyntsev
Author: this author's essay Two Hundred Years Together was panned as anti-Semitic; Elie Wiesel called him 'too intelligent' to be an anti-Semite
Author: this author's poem 'Prussian Nights' claims 'A girl's been turned into a woman / A woman turned into a corpse'
Author: this author composed his long poem Dorozhenka entirely from memory before writing any of it down
Author: in this author's story 'Matryona's Place,' the title character gives Kira timber from her dwelling, collected by drunken farmers using a stolen tractor
Author: this author designated stories about contrasting characters as 'binaries,' including 'Fracture Points' from Apricot Jam
Author: in the play The Love-Girl and the Innocent, Mereshcun's affair with Lyuba is discovered by Nemov
Author: this author advocated for a unified Rus' state encompassing Russia, Ukraine, Belarus, and Slavic Kazakhstan in Rebuilding Russia
